tools.name: Mimikatz
tools.evidence_snippet: Akira threat actors use tools like Mimikatz and LaZagne to dump credentials.

tools.name: LaZagne
tools.evidence_snippet: Akira threat actors use tools like Mimikatz and LaZagne to dump credentials.

cves.evidence_snippet: FBI and cybersecurity researchers observed Akira threat actors obtaining initial access to organizations through a virtual private network (VPN) service without multifactor authentication (MFA) configured, mostly using known Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs) [T1190], including: CVE-2020-3259 [Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE)-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor];

FBI and cybersecurity researchers observed Akira threat actors obtaining initial access to organizations through a virtual private network (VPN) service without multifactor authentication (MFA) configured, mostly using known Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs) [T1190], including: CVE-2023-20269 [CWE-288: Authentication Bypass Using an Alternate Path or Channel];

Akira threat actors likely abused Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVE)-2024-40766 (Common Weakness Enumeration [CWE]-284: Improper Access Control), a SonicWall vulnerability, for initial access.

ttps.evidence_snippet: Akira threat actors use PowerShell to execute malicious script
